text: the speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299792458 meters per second
atoms consist of protons neutrons and electrons held together by fundamental forces
the law of conservation of energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed
the periodic table organizes all known elements by atomic number and chemical properties
a light year is the distance light travels in one year roughly 9.46 trillion kilometers
the second law of thermodynamics states that entropy in an isolated system always increases
gravitational waves were first directly detected by LIGO in September 2015
quantum mechanics describes the behavior of matter and energy at subatomic scales
the Doppler effect causes a shift in observed frequency as a source moves toward or away
nuclear fusion powers the sun by combining hydrogen nuclei into helium at extreme temperatures
Bohr's model of the atom introduced discrete quantized energy levels for electrons
electromagnetic radiation encompasses radio waves microwaves infrared visible and ultraviolet light
the half-life of a radioactive isotope is the time required for half its atoms to decay
Newton's third law states that every action has an equal and opposite reaction
dark matter does not emit or absorb light yet constitutes most of the universe's mass
superconductivity occurs when certain materials lose all electrical resistance near absolute zero
the Higgs boson was confirmed at the Large Hadron Collider in July 2012
a black hole has a gravitational field so strong that nothing not even light can escape
the uncertainty principle states that position and momentum cannot both be precisely known
chemical bonds form when atoms share or transfer electrons to achieve stable configurations
plate tectonics explains the movement of lithospheric plates and the formation of mountains
absolute zero is the lowest possible temperature at minus 273.15 degrees Celsius
the wavelength of visible light ranges from approximately 380 to 740 nanometers
radioactive decay transforms unstable nuclei into more stable daughter nuclei over time
the strong nuclear force holds protons and neutrons together inside the atomic nucleus
Avogadro's number is approximately 6.022 times ten to the power of 23 particles per mole
thermodynamics studies the relationships between heat work and energy in physical systems
